text: I'm going to give the Etae line 4 stars. I can achieve a blow out using other products that are just as effective. They key to having straight hair, if you're not using Etae, is to shampoo once with a clarifying shampoo, then use a shampoo and conditioner that reviitize the hair. Use a heat protectant, leave-in conditioner to add moisture, blow dry and flat iron. Of course a good blow drier and flat iron are essential, as they save you time and less stress on the hair. Now, back to Etae. When I originally used this product, I thought it was a waste of money as my hair didn't look any different than my normal thermal straightening routine, plus it took more time with the extra step of letting the caramel sit for 20-30 minutes.  However, since I invested in the product, I am going to use it. It was 2 months or so before I used the product again. This time, I let the caramel sit on my hair for an hour while I cleaned around the house. Oh, and prior to the caramel, I did a pre-conditioning treatment to detangle my hair from a rod set. My hair felt different after rinsing the caramel. It was softer than I remembered from the 1st use. I applied heat protect spray and leave-in conditioner sparingly. At the end of the process, my hair felt soft and easy to blow dry and flat iron. Could it be the pre-Condish treatment? Because I modified the steps, I can't say 100% that Etae is 5 stars. Plus, I had a routine already. But, if you are new to thermal straightening I would give it a try.

text:
I do NOT recommend this product!! It contains a “special formula contains perfumer's alcohol and other proprietary ingredients” as stated in its description, which I overlooked! I’m sorry but if this product uses “proprietary ingredients” it is unacceptable! With people’s allergies and sensitivities to different chemicals (especially) I believe that some of the “proprietary ingredients” should be labeled!! Even extremely large corporations like C***ke for one, who do have a proprietary/ “secret” formula for their flavored syrup list ALL the other ingredients in their products! I realize that this item is from a small company, HOWEVER! This would have been used in conjunction with my essential oils to create my scented items for my customers, my friends, and my family! I CANNOT use and do NOT recommend a cosmetic component such as this specific product for use nor purchase!

I ended up using a 1:1 ratio of distilled water to high proof grain alcohol (173 proof/ approximately 76% alcohol) [the highest proof alcohol legal in my state, some states allow for higher proof vodkas and grain alcohols, the higher the proof, the better] with my oils in an amber, pump style bottle and just reminded people (and printed on label) to shake well before use.

I contacted Amazon and explained the situation, as the item is listed as “non- returnable”, (I believe due to its flammability [although it came via USPS!!!]) and received a refund from Amazon! Thank you Amazon for understanding!

text:
This is a very small amount of mud. The inside dimensions of the bottle are approximately 1.5” tall by less than 2” diameter. When I opened the bottle and removed the safety seal, the mud was to the top of the bottle. The directions include a step to stir before applying the mud. When I stirred it, I found there was a large air bubble and the bottle was only about half full. A little of this mud goes a long way, so this small amount will go further than some other facial masks.

As per the instructions, I washed and dried my face before application. The AntiAging MegaMud goes on very smoothly. It has a strong minty smell that becomes less overwhelming with time and is partially replaced with other smells that aren’t entirely pleasant. The smell isn’t bad, but it is not good either.

The mud made my skin sting immediately, but it became less intense until I washed off the dried mask. I had removed the mask and I felt my face burning still about 5-10 minutes later, so I washed my face again using cleanser. I was fortunate that there was only the burning sensation and not a significant amount of reddening.

The box says the peptides relax muscle tension by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters released by the brain. I'm not sure how I feel about having a topical beauty product interfering with my neurotransmitters. I'm probably getting new lines as my brow furrows trying to wrap my brain around this concept.

From the company website (https://www.viacosmedics.com/product/megamud/), this is a list of Ingredients: Deionized Water, Kaolin, Propylene Glycol, Montmorillonite (Bentonite), Glycerin USP, Magnesium Aluminum Silicate, Butylene Glycol, Carbomer, Polysorbate-20, Palmitoyl Oligopeptide, Palmitoyl Tetrapeptide-7, Acetyl Hexapeptide-8, Sodium Hyaluronate, Cucumis Sativus (Cucumber) Extract, Camelia Sinensis (Green Tea) Extract, Matricaria Recutita (Chamomile) Flower Extract, Symphytum Officinale (Comfrey) Extract, Ananas Sativus (Pineapple) Extract, Cucurbita Pepo (Pumpkin) Extract, Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Juice, Alpha Lipoic Acid, Algae Extract, Punica Granatum (Pomegranate) Extract, Calendula Officinalis (Marigold) Flower Extract, Argania Spinosa (Argan) Kernel Oil, Squalene, Eucalyptus Globulus (Eucalyptus) Leaf Oil, Phenoxyethanol, Caprylyl Glycol, Potassium Sorbate, Hexylene Glycol

From the other reviews, I thought I would like this product. After a certain age, many of us probably hope for that product that really will combat wrinkles and give us just a little more youthful appearance. I can’t imagine spending over 20 dollars for such a tiny amount of product that makes my skin burn and may or may not have anti-aging properties. For now, I’ll just have to settle for having my beauty come from within. Not such a bad thing anyway.

The good news is that this product was provided at a discounted price in exchange for my honest review.

text:
Top it off (gloss) is a big hit. It does a great job with sealing your gel manicure and lasting a long time. I appreciate that isn’t too thick and gloppy.

The matte top coat is an odd duck. It’s not suuuuper-matte, but it works really well for my purposes. I have a crazy nail (after a little accident) with a thin vertical strip that constantly splits. If you imagine that would be painful, you would be correct, so I cover that nail in clear gel polish. The matte-ness is pretty close to the sheen of a natural nail so it’s not immediately obvious unless you look super close (and even then). So I don’t have to wear nail polish if I don’t want to, but if I do, I can just paint regular polish over this. And if you use a non-acetone polish remover, you can even switch shades without this coming off.

The only reason I knock off a star is because the matte isn’t impervious, and it will pick up fabric dyes and other colorants fairly easily. Sometimes I’ll look down and notice that one nail is looking pretty grey (like the lenses on sunglasses), which is frustrating.

Take off is not too bad. Those crazy clips that hold cotton pads to your fingertips and some acetone will make short work of this in about 15 minutes with no scratching that damages your nail plate. People whine that gel is bad for your nails, but if I remove it properly, it doesn’t hurt them a bit, at least not in my experience. In fact, my nails grow pretty long and strong with this on.

text:
If you have used QuickBooks before, you already know what you are getting. The product works, it doesn't change much year to year, but I will say it has been more stable than in the past, with less crashing and weird issues. Desktop Pro 2019 has a few new features like invoice tracking, and checking for unpaid vendor bills but if you have 2017 or 2018 you don't really need to upgrade to be honest.

However, if you do want to upgrade and you are also looking to use QuickBooks for payroll, this is a decent option because it includes one year of Payroll Enhanced. The service itself is easy to use and ties in to QuickBooks directly, which is the main reason to use it in my opinion. The service has to be activated but once you do it, you are good for a year. Even a novice can start paying employees, and I found the payroll software to be easy to use once I got it all set up and running.

I'm giving this software 3 stars because although it works, Intuit always seems to do the bare minimum it can get away with and still call it a new release. It's been like this for years and although the software is solid, the company and philosophy behind the way they market and sell their products is average at best. They keep trying to push people to QB Online, which is terribly unprofessional and doesn't work with a lot of commercial banking options. Quickbooks Desktop Pro is still the best and easiest option for a small to medium sized company, and anyone can use it with just a little training. Payroll Enhanced ties in to QB and is easy to use once you get through the setup process, which can take up to an hour. If you have more than 15-20 employees, I'd recommend going to an outside service for payroll although you can certainly use payroll enhanced, but it will cost you per employee to use and at some point it's less expensive to use a company like ADP to process payroll. I use QB every day and I've learned to tolerate its quirks. QB Desktop Pro 2019 is an improvement over 2017 and 2018, although it's a small one and only recommended if you are really looking for the few new features it adds.

text:
The "VITU Jojoba Lipstick Makeup with Vitamin and Antioxidants for Long Lasting and MatteTones 0.07 oz", is a very affordable lipstick. I like affordable products so much because if I really like them, I can purchase them over and over and not worry about breaking the bank. I did have a few issues with this lipstick though, it doesn't go on smoothly...you have to press harder than a typical lipstick to get the color on your lips.

It has jojoba in it but I found it drying when I used it and it doesn't have added scent or taste but it does have that lipstick taste and scent to it (it reminds me of lipstick my mom used in the late 80's that I would play with). It did not make my lips look juicy like the picture on the product page either, it's more matte and it has mica added to it so that it sparkles a little. It does transfer and it does wear off but it leaves a stain on your lips which is typical in red colors. Overall, I think it's not bad but not my favorite.

text:
Pro’s: Good packaging, pretty colours, good size.
Pro/Con: they take a LONG time to dissolve, they were still fizzing after I wanted to get out of the bath...like 20-30 minutes after I put them in, so now I add them as soon as I start the bath running. Pro or Con depending on how long you are prepared to soak before they completely dissolve.
Con’s: Scent appears to be almost the same across all 12 bombs. Scent is very weak. I could get a teeny tiny occasional whiff of ‘strawberry’ or ‘rose’ or ‘lavender’ but it could equally have been my imagination/wishful thinking, all basically have the same scent. I was also mildly allergic to all of them...just some slight tingling, which didn’t spoil my bathing experience too much, and it won’t stop me using them up...but yeah. They produce slight bubbles, but not much and any bubbles go away really quickly. I ended up adding a squirt of bubble bath and sometimes a few drops of my own essential oils to increase the bubbles and scent.
I wouldn’t purchase them again. They were disappointing. But I guess you get what you pay for, and these were cheap. Will try another brand next time.

text:
I'm the proud owner of a rather glorious handlebar moustache (though I say so myself). So when a 'stache wax or grooming product comes available on Vine I'll usually request it if it's anything in the neighborhood of what I usually like.

I like firm or strong hold waxes with significant tackiness. I won't even consider a wax unless it either states a strong hold or a tacky hold or both. As a reference my other favorite common varieties of moustache wax include (in order of preference descending):
Firehouse Moustache Wax - Tacky (Wacky Tacky)
The Vintage Grooming Company - Death Grip
Can You Handlebar - Primary

All of these are either exceptionally tacky or very strong in hold or both. I have tried many other waxes by various brands over the last several years but the ones above are my favorites and the ones that I feel are most worth mentioning in comparison to this product.

I would rate this wax as slightly less tacky than the Firehouse Moustache wax Tacky and slightly less stiffness than the Can You Handlebar Primary wax. Death Grip has it's own unique and weird properties (and I don't love the way it smells) so it is hard to direct compare :-)

As far as my general ranking I'd put this solidly in the top 5 waxes I've ever tried though, probably just under the three above and maaaybe 5th place after maybe Fisticuffs Strong Hold (which also has some pine resin for stickiness).

Unscented is good! Nothing worse than a cloying scent right up under your nose holes all day long!

Anyway, it has a strong hold for shaping if you like a strong curl as I do, combined with a tacky hold if you get a lot of stray and fly away hairs as I do. While my "go to" product is still Firehouse Tacky, and this has not replaced that, this is a worthy offering. The tree embossment on the wax is cute and I kinda dig that they give you the recipe.

The price point at the time of this writing is very fair. If you're in need of a good strong hold tacky wax I'd say give this product a shot!

text:
this has one of the highest wattages of all LED nail lamps so i figure it’ll work better than the 12v i already have. plus, the ones the salons use are too pricey and have less voltage. long story short, it seems to cure well, hardening the polish better than my 12v lamp. i’m really rough on my hands due to laundry, dishes, and constant hand washing so only time will tell. my only complaint so far is the bottom is really loose that it comes off easily. i was moving it from the counter to the table and the bottom fell right off.

UPDATE - it’s only been 3 days since i did my nails and they’re already starting to bubble. some have already started peeling. updated my review from 4 stars to 2. i wore gloves during dish washing so i hope the heat trapped inside the gloves wasn’t the cause. i always apply thin layers and cap the free edges so my technique is solid. i’m going to bite the bullet and spend the money on a professional lamp.

text:
I don't like writing bad reviews, but I felt like this thing rates one. I really can't return it for a refund because it is what they said it is and it does what it says it does. It just does it really poorly. The different pads are a nice touch, but they're useless if the motor doesn't spin them effectively.

I put 4 brand new AA batteries in it, and got in the shower. I selected a pad, turned it on and it spins at probably 160 RPM - between 2 and 3 revolutions each second. Which isn't bad, except that it slows way down if you put the motor under a load (such as pressing it to your skin). It then bogs down so badly that I could easily cover the same area on my body with a 99¢ scrubbie and do a better job.

So this is going straight into a yard sale. Save your money and buy a scrubbie and a back brush - at least they don't eat batteries.

text:
I got this shaver as a gift for my husband who normally uses those shavers where you keep the handles but have to constantly buy the disposable heads. I've always seen pictures of these types of razors in shaving commercials so I figured I would give him a little gift. And WOW! I really didn't expect it to go over as well as it did! I don't know a ton about razors but I do know when the man is happy with his.

As soon as I got it and gave it to him, he was a little reluctant to change his routine. But then I couldn't get it back from him even to do a review for it! He loves it! He says it gives him a much closer shave (probably due to the fact that there's an actual razor blade inside the shaver) and that, while it's a little weird shaving with just the edge of a razor (instead of having those disposable heads against his cheek) he's starting to prefer it because of the results he's getting.

And it's really easy to clean! You simply turn a knob in the handle and the shaver opens and you can take out the blade inside and give it a little rinse and you're done! My husband applies a little oil to make it go further but it's a less than 30 second job and he sets it to dry to be ready for the next use.

After his experience, I really recommend this shaver to anyone else in the market who may have the same experience as him! My husband is a very hard man to impress and the fact that this has immediately become his new favorite razor says a lot to me and I hope it does to you as well.

*Disclaimer: I received this product at a discount for my honest and unbiased feedback. I do not review anything I wouldn't trust to use on my own body or on my family. My analysis is based on actual use and with the interests of the customer being my primary concern. Thank you for taking the time to read my review.

text:
I really wanted to like this product, but unfortunately, it was less than great quality, and one of the brushes I received was broken. Quite a few of the brushes had frayed bristles and had uneven thickness to them, which made even application hard to come by.

When I used some, it didn't seem like they blended the colors together well (mainly for eye shadow). I wish there were smaller round brushes for blending out shadows on your eyes because I feel like all the flat brushes are just about all the same. There is barely any size difference between three of them, and they can all be used for the same things so why not replace those with some rounder ones?

I tried to contact the seller about the problems I had with this product, but, after two days, I haven't heard anything back from them. I was waiting before putting this review up to see if there was anything they cared to do to help with these problems (new brush that's not broken, or a new set all together that has better quality to them), but I have gotten no response back from them to lead me to believe that they're willing to do anything to help.

I have attached photos to this post to show the problems I got with these brushes. The broken one was a foundation brush so I didn't get to try that one out to even see if it applied the foundation well, so I was a little disappointed with that. The larger powder brush seemed to do ok, but it didn't pick up powder that well. The larger fan brush I tried to use for contouring (I saw someone use a large fan brush for this purpose in a tutorial so I wanted to try it out), but even though the brush picked up the color, it didn't seem to apply it very much at all. The contour powder I used is super pigmented so I shouldn't have had that problem. The eye shadow brush I used (one of the smaller tapered brushes) actually worked pretty well so I don't have complaints about any of those except that some of the smaller brushes had frayed bristles, too. The smaller fan brush I used for highlight, and, though it did well with applying it, the unevenness of the bristles made it apply in a weird and uneven way, One of the larger tapered brushes I used to pack on a setting powder, but again, it didn't seem to pick it up well and left a ton of fall out once it hit my skin. I understand setting powder typically leaves fall out, but I've never experience so much before using this brush.

Basically, the best brushes in this set were probably the eye shadow brushes.

I hope this review helps give some insight on this product. I also hope the seller finds a way to fix the problems I had for future customers.

I received this product at a discounted price for my 100% honest review. I tried contacting the seller (as noted above) before leaving this review since it's a poor one, but I made sure to leave enough information to work with and to help others decide whether or not they would like to purchase this product.

text:
This past summer my family and I spent two weeks in Morocco. During that time we visited women's cooperatives that were producing argan oil products near the beach town of Essouirra. Of course, I bought some products. Upon our return to the United States, I was disappointed to find that I could not purchase argan oil beauty products domestically. Then, to my great surprise I found that one company--Xandra Renouvelle--had become the sole distributor of Kaeline argan oil products in the US. Since August I have NOT stopped using Kaeline products. The Kaeline argan oil products have reduced the number of perimenopausal breakouts that I've experienced each month. I've used Kaeline argan oil on my occasional cold sores and have found it more effective than the more popular cold sore medications. Kaeline has also improved the texture of my hair and provided desparately needed moisture to my arms and legs.

I support Kaeline's mission in that it derives its argan oil from wholly owned and run women's cooperatives in Morocco. These cooperatives are designed to provide income to women, many of whom are single mothers or widows.

Kaeline is certified by EcoCert, a European agency that supports organic and fair trade products.

Since discovering discovering argan oil, I have remained a faithful customer of Kaeline.

text:
Okay so I have used this product more than a dozen times more than a year. I love it. Works great for my eye brows. Here is what I have learned:
1. It can stain your counters/sink. Put a towel or rag down. If you do get it on your counters clean immediately. Soft scrub works well!
2. It doesn’t stain your skin. Don’t worry about trying to be neat about it and being detailed to your eye brow. Just slap that crap all over and set your phone timer for however long you want for how dark you want (I prefer 3 mins for a dark brown)
3. Set up. Mixing in that little bowl can be messy. I like to use a Q tip to slowly mix it. This way it does not get everywhere on my brush and I make sure I don’t leave any unmixed powder on my brush when I apply to my face.
4. Clean up. Cleaning that bowl again don’t want to get on the counters when you have extra left in the bowl. I use a baby wipe to get the extra out before rinsing the bowl and brush. This way I make sure my counters stay unstained!

4. clean up. cleaning that bowl again don’t want to get on the counters when you have extra left in the bowl. i use a baby wipe to get the extra out before rinsing the bowl and brush. this way i make sure my counters stay unstained!

text:
So far this serum is great! I'll come back and edit it after more prolonged use -- I hope that the long term effects will be good!

I have very sensitive skin and my pores get easily clogged. Honestly I cannot use 80% of skincare products out there and it takes me forever to find something effective yet gentle! This serum rally boosts moisturizing of dry spots and I think it's even smoothing out some of my skin's surface irregularity!! It's a little too soon to be sure but I'm really really hopeful >.<

The serum is super light, smells mildly medicinal (but I prefer that wayyy over putting artificially scented products on my face). It feels like water! I patch tested this product, which means I applied it to just a small part of my face for a while to test it. That's how I can tell of its smoothing effects; that part of my cheek feels soft, smooth, and bouncy. It's not a magical, radical, overnight change, but it's enough of an indication for me to fully integrate this product into my routine.
This product was recieved at discount for the purpose of review. ALl opinions are my own honest thoughts. I will update this review should my experience change.

text:
Mud masks are a new obsession of mine, so I've tried several different types and brands of mud masks in the past couple of months. Honestly, they're all pretty good at drawing the oils and impurities out of your skin, but I've found Dead Sea mud masks to be the best for me. This one is pretty good, as well, but not as awesome as some of the other ones I've tried. This Dead Sea mud mask definitely goes on super smooth with no grit. I love how easy it is to spread this on! It dries quickly, too, which is nice since other masks can take up to half an hour to dry. This one was ready in about 10 or 15 minutes. My skin did feel better after washing off the mask, just not quite as smooth and soft as I've experienced with other masks.
For the price, this is a great option because other brands can get pretty pricey. Luckily, I received this at a discounted price in exchange for my honest review.

This has got to be the BEST hair mask I have ever used hands down! I bought a sample packet of this at my local Sally’s Beauty supply shop. It was one of those “you have to spend $3 more to use your coupon” purchases. Thankfully I can gladly say that I was NOT disappointed with it at all. I am so happy to find that I can buy it in a bigger quantity!! It is the same price on here as it is in Sally’s!

Given that it is summer time and I swim almost every day with my kids, my hair was feeling and looking a hot mess! It was getting very frizzy and unmanageable with tons of little annoying fly always. After one use of this hair mask, my hair still looks and feels the best it has in years TWO days after using it!!

I would highly recommend this to anyone desperately searching for a miracle. The scent is ok, I typically like strong fruity smells, however this scent isn’t very strong or fruity. I’m not complying though because it left my hair so silky and beautiful! Definitely worth the money and with a large tub, you will get plenty of uses!

***Hopefully you don’t have the luck I did. When I ordered this I was surprised to find the container had busted open. The hair mask was EVERYWHERE! I put in for a replacement but they’re asking for me to send back the damaged item. Not really sure I can send it back to them like this....I’m hoping I don’t get charged twice. If that is the case, I will NOT order again. Very upset that I can’t use this tonight.***

text:
Oh Spring, how I hate thee... after a long night of terrible allergies and taking Benadryl, my skin felt so dehydrated! I have sensitive skin but had no problems with this mask. The ingredients are gentle but effective. It was light, moisturizing, gentle, and easy to use. This mask made my face feel so smooth afterwards and i felt like the moisture in my skin was replenished and it helped smooth out some of my fine lines. This is the perfect size face mask to take traveling! A little goes a long way.
I recently used this mask while traveling and it perked up my complexion beyond expectations. The pods are large too so allowed for two uses This cream really moisturizes and softens my face. There is plenty of cream in each pack and my face feels wonderfully clear after using it. These are spectacularly soothing and leave my skin soft and refreshed. You can also get at least two uses per pod. I would totally gifted these to my friends as they're a perfect pamper present. My husband is even a fan of these! Somedays I have such sensitive skin. I use this on my face, neck, and chest. It is sooooo soothing and helps with my skin which is prone to roscea if I'm not careful . After getting caught in the sun without adequate sunscreen, I wasn't burned, but my skin was on the border of disaster. This stuff calmed it down in an amazing way.

I know it wouldn't be too feasible, but I wanted to coat my entire body with this mask after I put it on my face. It feels so very indulgent and soft and wonderful! I enjoy using masks on occasion but I'm not too consistent with them. So amazing that this will be something I actually recommend to others.

text: My hair is naturally wavy but refuses to hold any kind of styled curl for longer than a few hours. This is hands down the best curling iron I've ever owned - the curls hold for 3 days without any product and would probably stay longer if I didn't have to wash my hair, and that's using the lowest heat setting. It's even low enough that if you accidentally bump it against your ear or your hand for a second it won't leave a burn, and it heats up in under 30 seconds. I used the black glove at first since I wasn't used to clipless irons, but it's kind of unnecessary, and makes it difficult to grab your hair. You could just practice the technique of wrapping your hair around it without turning it on and skip the glove entirely. Some reviews have said there's a weird smell, and there was a paper in the box that said something about there being oil around the heating fixture inside the iron that might make it smell weird at first but I haven't noticed anything.

text:
This is the first time that I've used a mask from this line. And I have to admit that the packaging influenced my purchase:)
I really like the feel of the serum and the light fragrance. I, unlike other reviewers, couldn't fit it over my ears. Also the eye holes were way too small. Tip: use the peeI off backing and laid it on my neck and chest.
Left it on for 10 minutes and when I removed it I have small bumps all over my face, but now after 30 minutes, they seem to be going away. Once the serum dries it does feel a little tight on your face but it does not dry out the skin. I finished with a rich moisturizer to "seal the deal." The next morning my face felt very soft but still had a few bumps around the mouth area. My advice would be for those who have dry, sensitive skin, might want to be weary of this mask. Not the mask's fault, mind you, just not a good match for my skin. I would have given it 5 stars but took one off because of the bumps...

text:
To create fine, long lashes, this mascara does a pretty nice job. Lashes look longer and natural.

The primer has itsy-bitsy pieces of fiber in it that adhere to the lash and the comb aligns them to lengthen the lashes. The bits of fiber kind of freaked me out when I kept thinking there were individual lashes sticking to the primer comb. The fibers are part of the lengthening process. With that said, I'm still not sure I like the idea of putting bits of fiber so close to my eyes. The packaging says nothing about being ophthalmologist tested, hypoallergenic, etc., so I don't think I'd recommend this product to anyone with sensitive eyes. After wearing this mascara all day, my eyes begin itching, and it requires more than water to remove.

I really liked wearing this mascara as my eyelashes looked longer and feathery and very natural, but I'm concerned about the ingredients.

text:
I have tried many eyelash curlers and while most are sufficient and get the job done I can honestly say this is my favorite eyelash curler along with the whiplash curler (by crave naturals also available on amazon).

I read once on in a beauty magazine that eyelash curlers tend to wear out after about a year and a half. No way!! A good eyelash curler with proper care (not getting dropped or crushed in a makeup bag) should last a lifetime, the pads are another story...but not with this one!.
I have tried the famous Shu Uemura and while that did a very nice job of getting a natural curl I could tell statement about wearing out after a year and a half as the metal connection did not seem very strong. This curler however is rock solid in it's build! The metal is thick and reinforced as is the connection. Another thing I preferred over the Shu Uemura is that this is strongly spring loaded where as Shu Uemura isn't.

The finger holds are very comfortable and smooth plus, also importantly, the curler looks classy! The pad on this curler, as said by the company doesn't need to be replaced. This is impressive as you can tell by looking at it and feeling it the pad is thick and does not have a lot of give to it but is still gentle enough not to harm your lashes.

As for curling. I get the curler as close to the roots as possible by about half opening my eye and the no pinch design allows you to do this easily. Then I hold for about 7 seconds occasionally pumping the curler. I then raise my eyebrows and move the curler a little farther down my lashes and pinch for a second for two while bend the curler backwards and raising the handle up a bit. The main issue with certain eyelash curlers is the over curled and bent lashes effect which also isn't good for your lashes. This curler, when not over pressed, gives a natural curler that is noticeable on it's own without even using mascara.

And a shout out to the wonderful company Bella and Bear with their wonderful customer service and wonderful products that they wholeheartedly back up! Can't go wrong here.

text:
I have my own gel nail kit at home and prefer to paint my own nails rather than go to the salon. I hadn't done gel nails in months because of how tedious and time consuming it was to soak my nails to change polish when my nails grew out, but this product has actually gotten me back in gel. All you have to do is file off your top coat, add the polish remover, wait a few minutes, and the polish with just slide right off! I love it too because I don't have to worry about damaging my nails using a drill to get the polish off either.
The product is safe, almost scentless, and completely painless to use. The price for 2 bottles of this product was actually less than the cost of one bottle of the competitors! I am definitely planning on buying this again if my bottle ever runs out. I've used it 4-5 times now and I still have well over half of the bottle left!

text:
I've been using this for four years now. Don't do as they say, do as I say and you'll be as happy as I am with it.

Don't charge/clean it every day. If you want your battery to last, do what I do! Only clean it in the Jet Clean when your battery level is at the bottom bar. Tap out cuttings once in the middle of the cycle and once just before cleaning. In fact, run it under the sink before you clean it. Your cleaning solution will last a very long time and you'll only need to clean the solution tray once when you change the fluid. The nice thing about the tank is you can pull it out to check it. When it's about empty, clean the tray and refill the tank. I've only used 6 bottles of cleaning fluid in 4 years.

The cleaning solution also lubricates the blades and it works! After two years, I bought new blades and there was NO DIFFERENCE to the old ones I replaced just because of their age. I wasted my money, the Jet Clean lubrication works that good, even only using it between full battery charges.

Also, after four years, my battery life is still around two weeks. You won't get this type of battery life if you charge this thing in the cleaner every day. Obviously, don't EVER just leave this thing in the charger as it will ruin the batteries. Besides, the blades won't dry out there anyway.

Also, don't, and I repeat DON'T EVER get the cleaner/ charger circuitry wet. If you do, give it two days to dry out before you plug it in again or the cleaner will be quite erratic in it's operation and could quite possibly burn out.

I've had four Norelco razors in my lifetime and this is BY FAR the best! When the new models came out, I actually saw no need to upgrade. For me? That's saying something!

Good luck folks, Danny

text:
Things to note: I waited 5 months to review the hair so I could give a fully informed review. I've colored, curled, cut, styled, washed, deep conditioned, blow dryed, you name it! Here's my review:

Pros-
• Affordable hair, 30+ inches will definitely cost more elsewhere, upwards of $350
• Takes coloring very well initially, I've only used watercolor meathod on any of my 613 hair.
• Straightens without much effort
• Holds curl but ONLY with flexi rods and mousse applied
• True to length with no odor or bugs after inspection
• Hair retained similar quality after being washed with swimmers shampoo (to remove gold tone/clean hair).

Cons-
• After 2-3 months of consistent wear, brushing and styling (Heat used sparingly, always with heat protectant) hair sheds uncontrollably. We're talking hair all 9ver every place I've stepped foot inside of.
• Hair will not hold heated curls, they fall within minutes
• Hair tangles and matts fairly easily in the nape
• Tons of split ends resulting in stragly looking hairs everywhere
• This is NOT 100% human hair, it is mixed with synthetic fibers, (I did do the burn test to confirm my suspicions).

Overall if you are looking for something cheap and quick with minimal styling, this is it..
If you want an investment, hair you'll be able to revive and re-wear for a few months, baby, this ain't it. I'd recommend this hair for an event but not for a unit/ like in you want to get the most out of. Hair performed as expected for the price.

I’m really tired of feeling like I’m wearing an old Halloween wig or something. I only got one month out of these bundles ($300 worth) and I’m very upset. This is also my first time wear a sew in and I feel like it ruined my experience. I can’t wait to get this hair out of my head.

text:
Pros- Nice colors, Good price
Cons- VERY Sticky, drying, have to be careful when applying, long drying time, do not 100 percent completely dry
Recommendation- I would recommend these to a first timer in Matte lipsticks
When i say have to be careful with applying I mean you can't just put it on and go out the door. Do not smack your lips together at all and be a little generous when applying. I recommend a heavy first coat but don't use two or it won't look right. It also takes a long time to dry ( 30 minutes, maybe a little more) and if you use more then one coat they will not completely dry. Maybe its just my lips but these are not easy to apply for me. This is just as a heads up to anyone who wants to buy as they are really pretty colors and a good price. I also said I recommend these to a first timer when it comes to matte lipsticks so you would know if you like the look and feel of matte lipsticks, without blowing a bunch of money on the really good high priced ones.

text:
A word of caution: if you have very sensitive or thin skin then this will leave marks for a day or so. My skin is very thin, so popping a pimple will leave a red mark for about a solid day normally. I still use this though, but I plan around work and social outings.

OK, now, the directions will tell you which tips to use with which suction levels. Follow them. Certain tips are very painful with the wrong suction levels. I can use the broad tips on max suction for stubborn blackheads just fine, but the pinpoint tip on low is too much for me. Odd.

Anyway, you are only reading this to see if it works. So, does it? YES! It actually does. I pulled all kinds of gross "stuff" off the filter inside that it sucked from my skin. I only used it around my nose to test it, as I have a bunch of those really tiny blackheads that look like freckles that I wanted to get rid of. One single treatment after washing my face with my normal acne routine and more than half the blackheads are gone. The empty pores are larger now though, and I can still see them but they look so much less noticeable than they were before.

I found that using small circles worked best and hitting the same spot over and over again. Only one popped like you can see online, the rest didn't really look like anything special while I was using it, but afterwards my skin was definitely cleaner and the screen was no longer clean. So yes, it is doing something and makes a difference.

I still use my regular acne routine to keep the oil at bay, but I am going to keep using this once a month to finish cleaning out all the pores. I only charge it every other use and even then it is not dead, I just don't want it to die while I am using it. Takes about an hour to charge. Comes with a nice little drawstring bag to hold everything in there and extra filters. I use alcohol wipes to clean them off and the tips as well. I folded up the instructions and shoved them in there too for reference.

I would recommend this, but with caution. It WILL leave red marks so watch out. People with thin skin need to be really careful when you start out so that you don't look like a racoon for several days. If used correctly it will do it's job and you can cover any extra redness with some concealer just fine. Or at least, that is my experience.

I buy a LOT of items from Amazon. Only about 10% of the time do I feel compelled to write a review. I've looked to buy an anti-fatigue mat for a couple years now. I finally bought two of these for our kitchen. It's hard to put into words how amazing these feel. Although it would look odd, I wish our whole downstairs was floored with this stuff. I walk into the kitchen now, and I find myself looking for an excuse to walk on them. "Can someone do the dishes?" ME ME ME!!!

I shopped around and looked at a ton of anti-fatigue mats. I finally settled on these because (1) outstanding reviews, (2) even though they're more expensive than others, I took a chance on the adage that "you get what you pay for" (they come with a 20 year warranty), (3) there is no design imprinted on the top (thus, dirt cannot get trapped in any grooves). I only want to buy such mats once. These were pricey, yes, but I don't want to buy a cheaper one that wears out in a couple years and then have to buy a new set.

All in all, these are outstanding.

text:
I purchased this Coconut and Retinol Anti-Aging Facial Serum because it promotes to being able to say goodbye to forehead wrinkles, crow's feet, turkey necks, and laugh lines. At the age of 55 I am beginning to see all of these, but I was mainly concerned about my crow’s feet and turkey neck. Just the names sound awful, but to me they looked a lot worse before I began using it. I have been using this product for just under 2 weeks and I am seeing a difference. I am using it daily and I do notice that the lines/wrinkles are smoothing out. I do not know if they will all come right back if I stopped but I like the results so far and will continue using it. I also read another reviewer where she was applying it to her spots/freckles and I thought why not try it and the spots are definitely lighter. I like the results that I am seeing so far and will continue to use it.

Another big plus is that the Anti-Aging Serum only contains four ingredients, simple and pure; one is coconut and the other is Retinol. There are no harmful chemicals used in this product and it is made in the USA.

I did receive this product at a discounted price for my unbiased and honest review and testing and am very happy with this purchase. All opinions stated are 100% my own and have not been swayed.

As a recent college graduate I've been noticing the fine lines on my forehead and eye area from late nights of studying/weekends. My skin has been getting drier as well. I wanted to find something to smoothen out my skin, moisturize, and serve as a preventative measure for signs of aging. The lotion is very thick.

Pros:
-when applied at night, you wake up with radiant-looking skin. Best to use after exfoliating too
-great body lotion for dry skin, morning and night. Although avoid sun exposure if using during the day due to vitamin C concentration

Cons:
-too thick and shiny to use on the face during the day
-feels heavy
-smells like grandma's face cream

text:
Quite a while back my Remington Microscreen needed the screen replaced, and for a few dollars more than the cost of the screen I bought Norelco's bottom-end cordless. It was under-powered, seemed like it was always at the end of the 30-minute shave time, and didn't cut well at all. All of the people I know who use a Norelco love them, and said that in Summer you won't get the razor burn on your neck you get with a screen razor, so I bought the 8894XL.
The shave is good, and when set to the softest setting the heads easily hug your face. (A firmer setting caused some razor burn, noticeable in hot weather only.) It's amazingly quite and powerful, shaves quickly, and cleans up easily. It doesn't shave as close as the Microscreen, nor as well my dad's corded Norelco of 15 years, which he almost never changes blades on. I have naturally wavy and very thick hair, which it misses occasionally, but is nothing to complain about. A quick shave only takes 3 minutes; a detailed shave takes 7. It takes some time to trim around high sideburns accurately and the area under the nose, and is useless for trimming extra eyebrow hair, all of which the screen razor does well.
As far as features go, they're great. It really does charge in an hour and last 100 minutes. I let mine go past the 0 minutes left warning the first few charges until the motor slowed regularly while shaving and then recharged it. (It automatically turns off when the motor can't stay close to normal speed.--recharge before this.) It lasts about 170 minutes, and the readout gets closer to the actual run time each charge cycle. Once you set the firmness to what you want it doesn't need to be adjusted again. The pop-up trimmer could be better, and is kind of like a plastic knife that's hard to cut yourself or anything else with, and doesn't bog the motor. The clear LED cover scratches amazingly easily, so you'll want to keep the razor in the thin bag when not in use. The cleaning indicator is accurate, and rinsing under hot water, blowing the water out of the inside, and drying the outside with a towel is quick and easy. Just keep the trimmer extended until the razor air-dries completely. I haven't heard any complaints about the battery other than online, so it may be the problem is fixed in the newer razors.
In summary, it's a fun razor that won't wake the family early mornings while giving you a quick shave. There's no razor burn when the firmness is set correctly, but it also won't shave as close as a blade or screen razor. There's no guessing on how long the battery will last, which is at least 100 minutes.

text:
GLO Science Teeth Whitening Pen 35 Aplications, Safe and Efective, No Sensitivity, Easy to Use Dentist Invented Whitening Treatment, Travel-Friendly

Are my teeth whiter? Yes. It's not so white that anyone will see me and be like 'wow your teeth are so white!' but it is whiter and I notice it, which I prefer. I have very sensitive teeth and almost no enamel. The pen is a click pen, and you have click quite a lot to get it started. I thought mine was faulty at first, and the clicker even got stuck half way down but I smacked it and it popped back and I kept clicking. The silicone tip has little bristles and it does a good job of coming off on your teeth - I was worried the gel wouldn't grip onto my teeth and would slide, but it didn't. It does have a cinnamon taste, and it does dry in 30 seconds, it was easy enough to smile for 30 seconds and it was on there. Truly I forgot about it after that (don't eat or drink for 30 min if you can). Are my teeth whiter? I couldn't tell after 1 use, but after two, I also couldn't tell... BUT I took a before and after photo (which I'll save you from because my lips look awful right now) and my teeth are definitely visibly whiter! I'm happy with this, it's easy to put on, doesn't make my teeth sensitive, works for whitening, dries quickly, and you don't even have to spit it out.

text:
I replaced a different manufacturer's very old electric razor with the Arcitec 1050, and have been using it for about three weeks. My primary shaving method has been the razor blade, supplemented by the electric for a fast shave now and then. I am so satisfied with the new Norelco that it has become my primary shaver. It delivers results virtually as close as a blade, and sometimes I think it is just as good. I don't feel that I need to use a blade to get a close shave any more, and I'm really amazed by the results. I didn't think that an electric could ever come this close to a blade. I guess that the technology has improved over the last twenty years, like everything else!

The shaver is not perfect, but the disadvantages are minor in my opinion. It does produce hair shavings, so I just shave over the sink. I don't mind cleaning up a little. The trimmer is quite small, but it does work well enough to clean the sideburns with an up and down motion. I have found that the shave is quite comfortable if you don't overdo it - just shave until you are clean, and use an after-shave cream to avoid minor irritation. I would prefer being able to leave the plug in the shaver, rather than remove it right after the charge is complete. It's a small inconvenience for such a great shave. And it is more economical than blades in the long run.

text:
I was immediately enchanted with this hairpiece from the second I tried it on. I've tried wearing it a few days in a row now, and I'm not tired of it yet. First, I'll start by saying that I ordered it as an add-on item in "dark blue to peacock green." With Prime, it only took two days to get to my house; very fast and convenient shipping, and the hairpiece was encased in a clear plastic bag; I threw that away, as I keep my extensions in a silk hair case. This hairpiece is actually pretty high quality for synthetic, eccentric hair, so I would suggest something like that to keep it safe and untangled when you're not wearing it.

In my opinion, the product images do not really do this hairpiece justice. I can't say anything for other colors yet, but the dark blue to peacock green is pretty true to the product images, with sapphire blue roots and aqua-blue tips (although I will say the hairpiece I received is not half-and-half blue and green like the picture; mine is like an ombre gradient, approximately 2/3 blue and 1/3 aqua tips).

Many of the reviews here also have mixed comments on the thickness of the hairpiece; maybe the people who say it is "thin" are comparing it to their own natural hair? I will say that this piece is very thick for a single-weft hair extension. A weft is like a curtain of hair with a seam holding it all together at the top. I wear extensions almost every day after a butchered haircut took 12 inches of my hair. Authentic human hair extensions come in wefts of varying widths, usually one clip wide or four clips wide, and the hair is very thin because the wefts are meant to be layered on in sections, like natural hair. This blue hair extension is 5 clips wide--wide enough to fasten on your head from ear to ear. Because of my experiences with authentic human hair extensions, I was expecting this blue piece to be thin and underwhelming, but I was wrong and am very happy about that.

I also have an asymmetrical haircut that is close-cropped on one side, so this extension is actually too wide for me to wear across my nape (as the model does in the product image), but I am still able to use it. I curve it to clip from my left nape to above my right ear and it suits my needs and still looks real, despite its unusual color.

Since there is so much hair on a single weft, as other reviewers have stated, this hair extension is a bit heavy. That may take some getting used to, as improper clipping could cause some discomfort and irritation from pulling on the roots. However, once you get practice putting it in, you will learn the best way to clip it without any tugging or pulling of the hair.

It is synthetic material, and while I noticed that mine was shiny, it was not like "Barbie hair," as some reviewers said. I did not need to rub any baby powder in; I just wore it to work for a few hours a day and the shine eventually dulled to look a little less like synthetic material. I will try to add some photos to this page of what the extension looks like in my (straight black) hair. I also noticed that it became easily tangled after a few days of wear. This is not really a problem; I am afraid to brush it too often though, as it sheds (which is normal, but I want it to last).

Lastly, despite being synthetic material, I have tried straightening it on the high setting on my Conair flatiron with success. You can also use a razor or scissors to make any modifications to the style or length, if necessary.

Overall, I think you will be very satisfied with this product if you buy it. I liked it so much that I placed an order for dark pink/light pink and yellow/lime green. These were not add-on items, however, so I will be waiting until mid-August to September to receive these other hair extensions. Hopefully they will be as nice as the blue/peacock green, because I was not disappointed!

in my opinion, the product images do not really do this hairpiece justice. i can't say anything for other colors yet, but the dark blue to peacock green is pretty true to the product images, with sapphire blue roots and aqua-blue tips (although i will say the hairpiece i received is not half-and-half blue and green like the picture; mine is like an ombre gradient, approximately 2/3 blue and 1/3 aqua tips).

many of the reviews here also have mixed comments on the thickness of the hairpiece; maybe the people who say it is "thin" are comparing it to their own natural hair? i will say that this piece is very thick for a single-weft hair extension. a weft is like a curtain of hair with a seam holding it all together at the top. i wear extensions almost every day after a butchered haircut took 12 inches of my hair. authentic human hair extensions come in wefts of varying widths, usually one clip wide or four clips wide, and the hair is very thin because the wefts are meant to be layered on in sections, like natural hair. this blue hair extension is 5 clips wide--wide enough to fasten on your head from ear to ear. because of my experiences with authentic human hair extensions, i was expecting this blue piece to be thin and underwhelming, but i was wrong and am very happy about that.

i also have an asymmetrical haircut that is close-cropped on one side, so this extension is actually too wide for me to wear across my nape (as the model does in the product image), but i am still able to use it. i curve it to clip from my left nape to above my right ear and it suits my needs and still looks real, despite its unusual color.

since there is so much hair on a single weft, as other reviewers have stated, this hair extension is a bit heavy. that may take some getting used to, as improper clipping could cause some discomfort and irritation from pulling on the roots. however, once you get practice putting it in, you will learn the best way to clip it without any tugging or pulling of the hair.

it is synthetic material, and while i noticed that mine was shiny, it was not like "barbie hair," as some reviewers said. i did not need to rub any baby powder in; i just wore it to work for a few hours a day and the shine eventually dulled to look a little less like synthetic material. i will try to add some photos to this page of what the extension looks like in my (straight black) hair. i also noticed that it became easily tangled after a few days of wear. this is not really a problem; i am afraid to brush it too often though, as it sheds (which is normal, but i want it to last).

lastly, despite being synthetic material, i have tried straightening it on the high setting on my conair flatiron with success. you can also use a razor or scissors to make any modifications to the style or length, if necessary.

overall, i think you will be very satisfied with this product if you buy it. i liked it so much that i placed an order for dark pink/light pink and yellow/lime green. these were not add-on items, however, so i will be waiting until mid-august to september to receive these other hair extensions. hopefully they will be as nice as the blue/peacock green, because i was not disappointed!

text:
The wax warmer is very color pleasing and so are the bars of wax that go inside the warmer itself. It comes with two roll on wax bars, along with finishing wipes (just oil towel) and a pack of paper. The directions are very easy to read on how to use the wax warmer but the pack of papers is awful in the direction department. So many spelling/grammar errors - but the pictures are more explanatory then the actual words themselves.

It took about 5-8 minutes for the wax to melt and be at a good use of temperature. I would wait until MAX temperature and then wait a few more minutes for the wax to go on smoother. Then slap the paper on.

Now, you are wondering why 3 stars?

1. The time it takes to warm up - 1 Star
2. The materials that are given plus directions - 2 Star

If you are doing a spot on shave sure go ahead but to do your entire leg and not to mention BOTH of them, you will be sitting for a very long time. Its too time consuming and I am impatience. I would suggest to buy more papers if you are thinking about getting this. I do feel that it is a learning curve and that is ok, I will continue to learn how to do it.

It does work though, works decently well once the wax is at the perfect temperature. Oh its not painless, but it is tolerable if you have a high pain tolerance, but if you DO NOT, I would suggest waxing to not be your thing.

text:
I just finished nursing my child and wanted to take care of myself starting with skincare. I had acnes back in highschool. I tried multiple laser treatments and it did very little to clear up my acnes scars. Plus one laser treatment cost atleast $500 and they quoted that I would need about 10 sessions to clear up my skin. That would be $5,000! So I did 3 laser sessions which had very little result and tried to do home treatment such as salicylic peel, retin a and dermaroller.
I strongly advise not to use salicylic or other peels at home as I burnt my skin and have permanent scar from it.

Retin a is good that it brighten up my skin but did little for my acnes scar or shrink pores. I tried dermaroller with vitamin c & hyaluronic serum but it really did nothing for my skin.

I read a skincare article that human stem cell containing EFG could reduce pore size & acnes scars. As I have tried many methods before, I was hesitant to purchase another item but just gave it another last try.
I searched and found Anterogen human stem cell skin pack. I read all the reviews and googled more information about stem cell skincare. There are two stem cell that is in the market. One is plant based( apple stem cell) vs human stem cell. You can google this yourself but all the articles state that plant based stem cell does nothing for your skin. It has to be human stem cell! So I purchased this brand as it had the highest amount of human stem cell.

I sanitized my dermaroller (1mm) in boiling hot water for 5 mins. I washed & dry my face then apply Anterogen white silk timeless machine serum on my skin. Then roll dermaroller roller over my face applying more serum on punched skin. Then finished it with this human stem cell mask. I left it on my skin for 30 mins. I also squeezed left over serum from mask on a facial bowl and tapped it on my skin and left.

The next morning, omg my pores &acnes scar finally looked smaller!! I literally teared up as I have found something that could help my skin look better. I am planning to do dermaroller every 2 weeks using Anterogen human stem cell serum with mask. Remember, don't over do it as skin needs downtime to recover and produce new collagen to fill up pores, scars or wrinkles.

text:
I've always been wary of electric razors, but I have to say this one works pretty well for me. It took no time for my face to get "used to" this razor (in the past it was hell to try to get through a couple of weeks with other kinds). The first day I used it I got a nice, close shave with no irritation.

A single charge lasts me a couple of weeks, so even though I've brought the charger with me when traveling I've never had to use it (I'll be leaving it home the next time). I haven't actually used the cleaning system yet, because you can run the entire razor under hot water and clean it out no problem. I just leave it on the counter to dry until I come home from work.

My only complaint would be that like most other people (and all other electric razors that I know about) it doesn't get all of those hard to get hairs on my neck without several passes, and the trimmer doesn't get close enough to my sideburns. Not a problem though, I just keep a disposable razor in the shower and touch up---just a few seconds with no lather so not a big deal.

I would recommend this product to anyone looking to make the switch.

text: Some of the most incredible stuff I have ever come by in my life! And this is why,....  FYI, I am from the Caribbean and we have a very great aloe vera, and I am not a stranger to this stuff! Anyway, I originally bought this due to the fact that I suffer from acne, and breakouts and redness and blemishes, lots of irritation due to the fact that I have extremely sensitive skin.  I was very careful, and read that one person used this on their acne and it really did help. I went on a whim, because I have literally spent about $200 in products because I had broken out for two months!  And after the break out, the redness and irritation would not go away… This  rarely ever happens to me for this long, if it does it happens due to stress and only for a short time. I won't have to use any products either, I just use proactive. But not even that was working! I didn't know what to do 😭 I even bought a facial steamer in which I would literally cleanse my face every few hours with just to open my pores and get the oil out, it would get better but then get irritated and red again.  That was the only thing that was helping so far! I do highly suggest buying one of those, they are extremely inexpensive, especially if you suffer from anything that I mention… Even if you don't suffer from it and just like to get a good cleanse, It was extraordinary. Then I came along this product! I just got it and as soon as I did, I quickly opened it and sprayed it on my skin to get a feel for the consistency. I do suffer from  sensitive but dry and oily skin, combinations. The worst… Anyway I remembered I had just cut myself, an actual legitimate cut from the knife that I had opened twice that day on my pinky and on my  Palm. I  sprayed the aloe vera on the cuts and it didn't sting too much but I knew that if did it and it was getting in there, it's not a bad thing. Not two minutes later did I already see results! And I'm not exaggerating either! (It really, really has to make a difference for me to actually go out of my way and  write a review about it, I must've bought 10,000 things and this is the only thing I have ever written for) The cut already looked more closed up, less pronounced, and the redness was already  lessening as well. I hit my pinky various times because I'm very clumsy and it never even opened up again! That's how I opened it up in the first place. I am so pleased, I am grateful. … I already put it on my face, and I was  definitely not expecting to see results this quickly either! The redness has already gone down and my skin feels so hydrated. It was becoming Incredibly dehydrated, then oily 😤  Now it just feels even, Way more balanced already. And it's not a cream! Hallelujah, that's the best part about it, and it dries so quickly.  Because I don't have to use so many products to go back and forth, and just flip-flop which is so unhealthy.  This stuff is mighty incredible, and I will keep buying only, And only from this company, and this product. Everything else I have bought has triggered my skin in the gnarliest in the worst ways possible, And they are very healthy because I have tried everything under the sun. Sometimes you just don't know what goes on… Not even my proactive worked  which I have been using for the past 10 years. I hope  this was thorough and helpful, I'm sure it will help for everything and anything in the world if it will help for the most sensitive things, especially the skin on your face. And it also helped for a very deep cut instantaneously, incredible stuff 5 stars

text: I bought a newer version of this dryer back in 2008. I LOVE it, if was still being made it would probably the best dryer on the market. It came with 3 attachments (diffuser, concentrator, and like a straightening pick). It has a boost button whic really does give your dryer a boost for the difficult to curl sections. It also is tourmaline, ionic, and ceramic. It has a durable AC motor, it's lightweight, and amazing. I prefer rocker switches because they are easier to change temperature while drying my hair. The flip down lint filter is another super convienant feature. The cord has a loop to hang my dryer from also. I am super sad that I lost my attachments in a move recently. I replaced my dryer with an infiniti pro and althought it's a nice dryer it is missing the boost feature, the third attachment, and the tourmaline I also prefer the ion shines flip lint catcher to the infiniti twist off one. The Infiniti has a narrower nozzle which is nice for control but sad because I can't interchange my attachments. I hope conair sells replacement attachments. I am giving them a call today. Overall I feel the motor is probably the same motor, it just seems like Infiniti pro is a skinnier version of this dryer minus some extra features.

text: Like so many reviewers, I also experienced skin irritation, redness, and peeling. I contacted the company and was effectively gaslit, directed to a page on their website that describes contact dermatitis. My experience with Myro deodorant seemed much more akin to a chemical burn, as others here have described. When I reached out to Myro, they offered absolutely nothing to try to make the situation right. I am beyond disappointed with my experience. Further, the product itself was a wet, melty mess that leaked out of the container into my bathroom cabinet on a daily basis. text:
While a sample set, the amount you receive is actually quite generous. There are two different shades of beige here for foundation, a very light 'all-purpose' powder, several eyeshadows, a bronzer, a package of travel-size brushes, and sheer setting powder. I would consider the eyeshadows full-size really and each jar is packed to full capacity, but does not have a little plastic cover on top so be careful when opening them!

It took some experimenting on my end to see what worked for me. With my skin tone, I have two different shades between summer and winter. My summer foundation is now too dark, so I purchased this because I go between light to medium depending on the sun/weather. What works for me is using the lightest foundation as a base, the medium beige as natural color 'bronzer' for normal sunkissed areas, then the all-purpose as a highlighter. The coverage is amazing for the price and hides my under eye circles quite well. I have not used the actual bronzer since it's rather dark and dramatic, but if you're into contouring like that, it should be fine. The finishing powder is nice as well, but I prefer a setting spray to get rid some of the drying effect of mineral make-up.

I also recommend you use a primer and a moisturizer beforehand, letting it dry for a bit before using the powders. My acne medication causes dry patches and the first attempt I did with just primer made it pretty obvious. I also didn't use the brushes because I have my own set as well.

text:
I also had never used a clamp-less iron before, but I have spent a lot of time trying to achieve that nice loose spiral that holds but doesn't look too rigid. This really does the trick. I have long,straight, baby fine hair and summer humidity is pretty much death to anything I try, and the curl falls right out. Somehow this holds up really well.

My complaints with it are just that even though it does get really, really hot, it seems to take longer to heat up than some of the "instant heat" irons I've been used to. So even though it doesn't take too much time once it's fully ready, there is a longer wait time on the front end, and not really a clear way of telling when it's fully ready either--it doesn't have a color-change light or dot like some of them do. Also, the glove is kind of dumb. I took a stab at it the first few times. It's weirdly small, for one thing--so it's awkward putting it on, you can still feel a good amount of heat through it, so it's not that effective at sparing your hand, and I could never get the hang of using it with the glove. It just felt really clumsy and took at least twice as long, so I stopped using it, and it's totally doable. You do have to be a little careful, sure--but honestly, I was hand wrapping my clamp curling iron almost the same way when I was trying to fake the spiral curl look so this isn't all that different---only this time it works. Now that I've had it for about a week, I feel like I have it really down. Since it takes longer, I can't use it every day during the morning rush, but it's kind of a fun way to shake things up.

text:
I have for years dealt with dry, cracked heels, and I have tried a number of things to correct the issue. While my heels haven’t hurt or anything, they have been ugly. In a search for solutions, I found this foot scrubber and decided it was likely going to work better for me than a foot rasp, and I ordered it. It’s a great tool and it works well, though I do offer some caution for those who haven’t worked with a foot scrubber or rasp in the past.

This is an odd-looking instrument, vaguely similar to a comma in shape. The scraping side somewhat resembles a mirror with a patterned rough edge. It feels a lot like an emery board though it has more of a “bite” to the surface, if that makes sense. The overall size and shape make it easy to grip and hold, even when wet.

The first time I used it, I scraped away a fairly large amount of dead skin, to the point that I had feeling in parts of my heels where I have had no feeling for quite some time. It left my heels soft, pink and looking much better. However, I did discover that one can overdo, and I had nicked myself on each heel in one or two places, just enough to require blotting but not enough to really be a concern (it was less serious than a cut while shaving).

I discovered the following day what happens when you remove too much dead skin and don’t put enough lotion on an area that deals with weight and stress – my heels split in a couple of places and I ended up having difficulty walking without pain for a couple of days. I had put high-quality lotion on my heels, but apparently not enough. That was entirely my mistake, and it was a painful one.

After that finally healed up, I tried again. I have fallen into a daily routine where I gently scrub my heels for a couple of minutes each, then apply lotion. My heels now look much better, they’re not splitting when I put weight on them, and I have avoided nicking myself. At least in my case this is not a tool that gets used intensely every couple of weeks, but rather it is a daily tool that gets used very gently.

I got curious and put this tool under a digital microscope to see what the rough surface looked like. The roughness resembles a series of crescents, not dissimilar to a rasp, but much more fine.

This thing really works and it does a good job. It’s slightly more expensive than the average foot rasp but it seems to work well. I do recommend using some caution when working with this instrument as it is possible to overdo if not careful, which is probably very true of any tool of this type.

text:
I have used the norelco wet/dry shaver for over 6 years now. I first used it in Iraq, it was a Godsend. Yes, it obviously takes longer to shave than with a regular razor to get a good shave, but shaving everyday with a regular razor destroys my face. Once you get used to this razor, I assure you you will practically look forward to shaving. I have found that using a face cleanser (like Phisoderm or others) along with the moisturizer you load in the razor make the shave much nicer. I am overseas again in support of Afghanistan and travel all over the place. This razor holds up during my travels with no issues. It is more durable than you think. 1 charge lasts about 5/6 days, depending on how long you take to shave. They are not expensive, so I own two of them to ensure I always have one charged.

Again, great razor and highly recommended.

LTC John K

text:
When I first opened this brush I was very excited to see how my spoiled cat would like a nice brushing with it. I pulled it out of the package and showed it to him as he needs to inspect everything before he will allow it near him. He unfortunately ran away as soon as he got one sniff! I was puzzled, but I quickly realized that the silicone was not smelling great. That sometimes happens with new silicone and it just needs to sit out for a few days to have the smell dissipate. In a couple days the smell went away and I gave the brush another try. At first my cat pulled away because he remembered the smell, but once he realized it didn't smell, he let me give him a pet. He generally likes gentle petting, but this brush by nature makes even the most gentle brushing a little more rough. He accepted the new level of brushing and I proceeded to brush him. I was immediately amazed at the amount of cat hair this brush was removing per brush. This hands down is the most efficient cat brush I have ever used! I had to clean the bristles every few strokes, which is very simple to do by peeling the hair off in one large section. My cat is a short hair and does not shed very much at all, so I was confused as to how so much hair was being removed from my cat. He was in heaven so I kept on going until each section I was brushing would not brush out any more hair. When he got up from brushing, the cat looked better to me. Maybe because all of the loose hair was no longer holding down his shiny Tuxedo hair. In all I took out a handful of hair from my 7 pound cat. His coat looks shinier, fluffier and healthier now because of a simple brushing.

One thing I do need to mention about this brush is that it is not the most comfortable thing to hold for long periods of time. I pet my cat for about 45 minutes and my hand was hurting from gripping the cube for so long.

I removed 1 star - for the initial smell and the uncomfortable hand grip.

text:
Short review: very versatile (I use on my baby's delicate skin and my chapped lips), rehydrates skin instantly, all natural ingredients.

Long Review:
First time mom here,

I absolutely love, love, love this salve! Every woman I spoke to about breast feeding in my family recommended lanolin cream but I knew I wanted to look for a natural alternative. I came across this company on instagram and decided to try them out as they were the least expensive of some of the other competitors. I have a flat nipple so the beginning of breast feeding was very uncomfortable. I made sure to apply the salve after each feeding and it surely works wonders. My nipples would be cracked and dry after feedings but after I put this on it instantly healed them. I love that I don't have to wipe it off before feedings. This salve is so versatile, I use it on my baby when she scratches herself and on her skin when it peels, I even use it on my lips as chapstick! This is honestly the best investment I've made for myself as a new mom!

text: This is the reasonably-priced refill for the ceramic jar (which is expensive). It lathers easily, with very rich and lubricating, very lightly scented, foam.  Yet, it rinses from a DE razor quite easily.  I favor the scent of the Taylor of Bond Street Sandalwood Soap, but this product is more lubricating.  This is an excellent product.

text:
I have been shaving with a brush and a mug for nearly 50 years. This is, without a question, the best shaving soap I have ever used (and I have used many different brands, cheap and expensive). The wool lanolin in this soap seems to be the key ingredient. The lather on my face stays wetter longer than any other shaving soap I have used. The result is a smooth, comfortable shave even after several days of beard growth. My only complaint is the scent, which is a bit too musky for my taste -- but it does not linger too long, so even that is OK.

An edit as of 8/15/16: I have now used two bars of this soap. Beyond the scent issue (I still do not like it), I find that the bars fall apart far sooner than similarly priced brands. Cracks appear in the bar about half way through its life and then rapidly yield multiple pieces. While these still work (sort of), they make an unsightly and unpleasant to use mess in my shaving mug. I have concluded (perhaps incorrectly) that this shaving soap is not hard milled, but simply pressed. I've never had hard milled soap perform this way. In any event, I'm looking for a new and better shaving soap.

text:
I have been using Odaban for about a year now and I do have to say it works. After suffering from embarrassing under arm sweat my whole life, this seems to be the only product that works. I do find that it burn quite a bit after application. What I do "for those considering this product". Is I make sure that I shower a couple hours before bed to make sure that my under arms are clean and dry. I apply before bed but with only 1-2 sprays per arm and then I rub it around the entire under arms to make sure It gets everywhere because I notice that if you miss a spot, that spot will sweat.

The burning does happen still, but not as bad. I never used this daily or weekly....but I notice that after a spray, it lasts for about a month for me. When I start to notice sweat, then I reapply and all is well!

text:
I shop at Amazon for extensions because local beauty supply stores don't ensure quality, shelf life or offer a money back guarantee. These bundles purchased 2 weeks ago are an example of what I usually get from beauty supply stores. Although this product had no (0) reviews, the ad clearly stated: "18 20 22 24 Inch Water Wave Bundles Brazilian Human Hair 4 Bundles 8A 100% Unprocessed Virgin Hair Extensions for Women 100g/bundles Brazilian Wet and Wavy Water Curly Human Hair Bundles Natural Black Color"
The hair felt silky and soft, and showed no tangles. But when I attempted to highlight 2 of the 4 bundles using 30 volume developer and NOTHING HAPPENED!! I snipped an inch and lit it with a match; it melted like plastic. There was my evidence.. In case I'm challenged I will gladly upload photos of this artificial hot mess!
I will not buy any more hair products unless it has more than 100+ reviews.
