text: I was not impressed with this foot mask. It claims to be exfoliating, so I expected it to work like other foot exfoliating masks I’ve used in the past. The other foot masks are used like these are - there’s a gel distributed inside some plastic booties. You open the top of the booties, put your feet in the booties, massage the gel through the bootie so your entire foot is covered with gel, tape the bootie closed around your ankle them sit back and relax for an hour or so. The other masks Ive used took about three days for the exfoliation to start - I would shed thin sheets of skin for several days and at the end be left with amazingly soft uncalloused feet. This did not happen with this product. No shedding, no exfoliation. My feet may have been a little softer immediately after washing off the product, but that is the only benefit I received. I thought maybe I didn’t do it right with the first set in this 3-pack, so a month later I got out another pair of booties, read the instructions super carefully, left on the booties for the longest recommenced time and waited to see if I’d get better results this time. Nope. Will go back to ordering one of my previous brands

text: I had collected several Barbie dolls and kept them in a container with some mothballs. All the dolls smelled horrible, I tried putting them in the sun and this worked O.K. but when I did some research on mothball smell I found Smelleze. I put the granules in a box and added two of the dolls and then put a couple of the dolls in a zip top plastic bag and added the granules. The next day I removed the dolls and they were better but still not great so I repeated the process, as the instructions indicated, and each time they got better and better and now they have no smell. I called the manufacturer with a question and the rep was very informative and helpful. I will order more of this for a trunk that smells of mothballs. I love this stuff.

text: This new razor replaces my Norelco Tripleheader Speedrazor Model HP-1615 that I purchased in 1985 and has given me superb service for all these years without having to be serviced. But it had been making some loud clacking sounds lately and it does not give the close shave it once had. I did not want a razor that required me to constantly spend money on special cleaners, etc. I did not have to spend one penny on my 1985 model after I purchased it. Although it is early, I have been using it for only three weeks, it has done a great job. The original charging of the battery was fast. I have yet to recharge it in spite of daily use. It gives a very fast and very quite shave. I tend to suffer from red neck syndrome from shaving, but so far no problem with this razor. I have it on the sensitive setting and I still receive a close shave. Since this razor comes directly from Amazon, there is no problem in receiving it as with a third party supplier (Amazon uses either USPS or UPS, the only consumer friendly shipping people). My final decision to purchase this razor came after reading all the reviews, which I find invaluable. Consumer magazines only review items such as razors only once every few years. Kudos to Amazon for making the whole process painless and allow me to obtain a quality product.

text:
Hi, It’s Glamorous Gem, hope you all are having a wonderful day,

Anyway, I always like a universal accessory that goes with just about anything chic and simple. This would definitely complete any outfit with those attributes.

I love the design. The silky texture is super cute and attractive, and the pearls really make these headbands pop. So elegant, smooth, and stylish.

These are also quite thick. There is silky fabric on the outside and thicker fabric on the inside surrounded by a metal band. There’s a single band on one side and three metal bars on the other lined with fabrics and pearls as well.

This doesn’t look or feel too cheap. They are above hair store quality and seem very sturdy. They fit on my medium sized head quite easily and with no slip. They also don’t seem super uncomfortable when wearing as well.

The only problem I had with these are that they are a bit smaller than I expected. I thought they would be wider to cover flyaways, but these are a bit slimmer than expected. But, they do seem cool and unique. And, these are perfect for those that like to show their edges with a fresh new hairstyle like me.

So, all in all, these fit my recommendation for switching it up in spring. It’s always good to try something new.

PS: No matter the nature, I only write honest, unbiased, and authentic reviews. Thanks for reading🥰🥰🥰

text:
I just received this dermatoscope yesterday. For the price, it is excellent. It is a simple to use, contact dermatoscope that gives the user a bright, clear image of skin lesions. I like that it is LED since those seem to last forever, and it even has a lifetime guarantee on the light source. It is comfortable to hold and the carrying case fits well in my white coat.

My reason for not giving 5 stars is that the product description claims that it can be used with or without oil. I suppose this is technically true, as I use alcohol as my immersion fluid of choice. However, if this scope is used without any immersion fluid (as implied by "oil-free epiluminescence microscopy"), it is basically just a 10x magnifying glass. The purpose of dermoscopy is to help visualize structures below the stratum corneum. If the light is not polarized (which it is not in this dermatoscope), then an immersion fluid is necessary to reduce light reflection/refraction/scatter by tiny irregularities at the skin surface in order to see the deeper structures. If you want a true oil-free dermatoscope, you will need one with polarized light (note that these are generally much more expensive and do not necessarily produce a better image). I truthfully did not expect the claim "oil-free" to hold true, but purchased it anyway since I don't mind using immersion fluid and the price cannot be beat. Therefore, I'm not disappointed, but if you are considering purchasing this mostly due to the "oil-free" claim, I'd advise against it.

Basically, if you want a dermatoscope that will give you a great image at a great price, this is your product. If you do not want to use oil or another immersion fluid, make sure you purchase a dermatoscope that is polarized.

Update: October 2013 - After using my dermatoscope for three and a half years in residency and private practice dermatology, the contact plate (where the glass contacts the skin) broke. When I contacted the manufacturer they responded quickly and even offered to send me a free replacement. I continue to be pleased with this dermatoscope and the customer service that stands behind it.

text:
Alpacca is also named German Silver or Nickle Silver. Consisting mostly of copper, nickle and zinc content. In my brief research I find that Alpacca does not contain any actual silver. Though some may be silver plated. But do not think of it as cheap imitation silver. It is more akin to a subset of brass.

This Alpacca cane is the pricier big brother to the plastic headed walking stick of the same design. The plastic brethren of this stick was love at first sight. The flared head and modest fluting simply screams elegance. Nowhere have I found a more elegant walking stick for the 'top hat' era.

As reported with flared head sticks, it is better suited for show. The slightly rounded top makes putting any kind of real pressure uncomfortable without leather gloves. However, it does provide modest balance if weight bearing is not your walking issue. The silvery smooth head does not make the cane top heavy.

The rubber tip is pretty standard and is useful for standing the 3/4" wide cane on flat surfaces. On the negative side, it takes away from the distinctiveness and gives an "old man" quality. I ordered my cane through a vendor who does silver ferrules (plated brass tips) and upgraded the business end of this cane to become a "Gentleman's Walking Stick". Silver ferrules raise the value and elevate the distinctive beauty.

This Alpacca cane lacks the distinctive charm of a chrome collar. My vendor states he routinely heats up such heads for removal, but the extra cost hardly seems worth the upgrade. It was mostly due to removal of the head. Unlike the screw top plastic cane, the Alpacca head is hollow and intended for permanent mounting, using pitch.

As it stands, this cane is suited for mild assisted walking. Use it more for classy strolls, theater, special occasions and dancing. Do not forget the white leather gloves!

Addendum:
On Saturday, 2/7/2015 I finally revealed this walking stick for a big birthday celebration. My black and white clothing style makes me look good, but there is a "whoa" factor when seen with this stick and a pair of white leather gloves. It helps that I had the tip refined with a ferrule and small rubber tip. I love it.

text: So to start this review off I have to say the products do work well! Please be aware if you are a skin issue sufferer, and use sparingly. If you notice itching or burning, then we have something in common and do NOT continue use. I no longer use this product as it consistently causes me reactions and break out issues with my eczema. While they work well and do what they say,  something about this product doesn’t agree with me so I cannot continue to use it.

Color 3, 8, 613 used
I have had them in for 3 weeks. I ordered 3 packs at 18" long. I ended up only needing just over 2 packs ohh installed. They were beautiful and matched perfectly, which I was super pleased with. They hold up great when using hot tools on them and I have had no issues w the hair burning or singeing. Which supports the claim these are 100% humor hair. Mind you, these are color treated to achieve the balayage affect. They may frizz just a tad after showers or getting them wet. The quality exceeded what I expected, honestly, being they are very inexpensive. I had an issue with the tape being very itchy, which I had written about earlier. The company reached out to me with an apology and offered me a small reimbursement to compensate the cost for new tape. They customer service rep I emailed back and forth was very friendly and sincerely apologizitic regarding my issue. The customer service from these guys is truly wonderful and I was no expecting any outreach. However, they took time to try to fix my concerns and were quiet prompt. I'm not very pleased, because companies who actually care and accept there is room for improvement are few and far between. 100% will reorder from Fullshine when the time comes to replace these

text: I have a 2016 F-150 XLT with Sync 3 and the large 8" touchscreen. I've been using android phones and the android auto feature since I bought the truck. I just recently switched back to iPhone and after doing some research found that all you need to do to get CarPlay is switch out the USB module. I've seen these for as little as $40 but most on Amazon are about $60. Installation was simple. I just used a thin flathead screwdriver, popped out one end and used my keys to hold it open (if you don't put something in to hold the one side it will pop back in when you go to open the other side). Swapping out the connections was easy. Snapped it back in place, started up the truck, a new screen came on asking to tap ok for CarPlay and voila! I now have Apple CarPlay and it works great!.

text:
It's a saddle stool
I've never ridden a horse
Is this the feeling?

I went from sitting in a hard chair that I moved back and forth across the room from my computer to my reference books and back to this saddle stool. It is a definite improvement, although it did take me a few days to get used to the seat. At first I thought I had made a mistake as it felt really uncomfortable, but before too long I settled in to it and now it feels nice on my bum. It is much easier on the floor as well for me to roll from desk to desk instead of dragging a hard kitchen chair.

It takes up very little space (which is one of the reasons I went with this stool as opposed to a full-size rolling desk chair) and holds up to 550 lbs (I'm a large dude -- although not that large -- and wanted something that would fully support my weight). Lastly, it was an absolute breeze to put together.

text:
This product is perfection. I’ve tried many sunless tanners and did spray tans for a long time too. Here’s why this product is superior:
It’s cost effective. 25$ every 2-3 months for a consistent and easy tan. Doesn’t streak. You only need a few drops so a little goes a long way, so it will last forever. I use on Face and body and it works perfect for both. No blackheads. Color is very subtle and natural and you can layer it- I find about every 1-3 days keeps it consistent. I used it the last two days in a row and I feel like it’s a little dark so tomorrow I won’t use it. It doesn’t speckle or splotch as it fades it is very gradual. No French fry smell.
